info.title: Dio provider Creem credits Kimi K2.6 default

parts.text:
# Dio Provider, Creem Credits, and Kimi K2.6 Default

  ## Summary

  Add a reusable Dio provider module that makes dio the default web provider and kimi-k2.6 the default model, backed by https://bifrost.dio.computer through Bifrost virtual keys. Users without a Dio key see a purchase flow,
  pay for one configured Creem credit bundle, and receive a Bifrost virtual key that is automatically stored as the dio provider key.

  Use server-side Nitro routes for Creem and Bifrost secrets. Use Bifrost budgets as the credit enforcement layer. Use email-based recovery. Relevant docs checked: Creem checkout/webhooks and Bifrost virtual keys/OpenAI-
  compatible gateway.

  ## Key Changes

  - Add a local reusable module, web/src/dio-provider/, with browser/server-safe exports:
      - dioProviderDefinition: provider id dio, label Dio, base URL https://bifrost.dio.computer/v1, API openai-completions.
      - DIO_DEFAULT_MODEL: model id kimi-k2.6, name Kimi K2.6, provider dio, reasoning enabled, text input, unknown cost, large context fallback.
      - client helpers: getDioAccessStatus(), startDioCheckout(), claimDioAccess(), recoverDioAccess().
      - server helpers: Creem checkout creation, Creem webhook verification, Bifrost virtual-key creation, entitlement persistence.

  - Wire dio into existing provider/model seams:
      - Add Dio to getSelectableModels() ahead of built-in cloud providers.
      - Change DEFAULT_MODEL in the web chat stream layer to DIO_DEFAULT_MODEL.
      - Store/retrieve Dio virtual keys through existing getAppStorage().providerKeys under provider name dio.
      - Ensure shouldProxyModel() proxies Dio requests through /api/chat-proxy, preserving the user’s Dio virtual key as bearer auth.

  - Add a Dio-specific key prompt path:
      - For provider dio, replace the generic “paste API key” dialog with “Buy Dio credits” and “Recover access by email”.
      - After successful checkout/claim, save the returned virtual key automatically and retry the pending model request.
      - Still allow manual key paste behind an advanced/disclosure affordance for development and support.

  ## Payment and Provisioning Flow

  - Add Nitro routes:
      - POST /api/dio/checkout: creates a Creem checkout for the single configured product and stores a pending purchase record in useStorage("keating:dio").
      - POST /api/dio/webhook: verifies creem-signature, handles checkout.completed, ignores duplicate event IDs, validates product ID, creates or updates a Bifrost virtual key, and stores the entitlement by normalized
        customer email.

      - POST /api/dio/claim: accepts email plus checkout/purchase reference when available, returns the virtual key if the webhook has completed, otherwise returns pending.
      - POST /api/dio/recover: accepts email and returns the active virtual key for that email.

  - Bifrost virtual key creation:
      - Call POST https://bifrost.dio.computer/api/governance/virtual-keys with server-side BIFROST_API_KEY.
      - Include a name like keating:<email>:<purchaseId>, description/metadata linking Creem event/order/product IDs, allowed model kimi-k2.6, and a budget max equal to the purchased credit amount.
      - Store returned virtual_key.id and virtual_key.value; only return value to the browser after a valid claim/recovery.

  - Configuration:
      - CREEM_API_KEY, CREEM_WEBHOOK_SECRET, CREEM_PRODUCT_ID_DIO_CREDITS, DIO_CREDIT_BUDGET, BIFROST_API_KEY.
      - Local dev docs should mention export BIFROST_API_KEY="$(fnox get bifrost_api_key)"; never expose or fetch this key client-side.

  ## Tests

  - Unit tests for Dio module constants and model shape: provider dio, model kimi-k2.6, base URL normalization, selectable model ordering, and default model selection.
  - Server route tests for checkout payload validation, webhook signature verification, duplicate webhook idempotency, product mismatch rejection, and claim/recovery pending/success states.
  - Mocked Bifrost tests verifying virtual-key request shape and that only virtual_key.value is sent to the client.
  - UI tests for Dio API-key prompt behavior: missing key opens purchase dialog, successful claim stores provider key, recovery by email stores provider key, manual key fallback still works.
  - Run rtk just test-web, rtk bun run typecheck in web/, and rtk just build-all after implementation.

  ## Assumptions

  - V1 uses one Creem product configured by env, not hardcoded pricing or a live catalog.
  - Credits map directly to a Bifrost virtual-key budget; Keating does not maintain its own spend ledger.
  - Email is the durable account identifier; normalized lowercase email keys entitlements in Nitro storage.
  - The Bifrost gateway has or will have a routable model alias named exactly kimi-k2.6; if not, set a single module constant/env override during implementation without changing the public Dio provider interface.
  - Nitro useStorage("keating:dio") is acceptable for v1 persistence, matching the existing share-storage pattern. If the deployment storage is ephemeral, configure Nitro storage to a persistent backend before launch.

Let me start by exploring the project structure to understand how providers/models are currently wired in the web app, then implement the Dio provider module and its tests.

I'll create a todo list since this is a multi-step task.

todos.content: Explore web provider/model architecture and existing seams
Implement web/src/dio-provider/ module (browser/server-safe exports)
Wire dio into getSelectableModels(), DEFAULT_MODEL, providerKeys, chat-proxy
Add Dio-specific key prompt path with purchase/recovery/advanced paste
Add Nitro routes for checkout, webhook, claim, recover
Write unit tests for Dio module constants/model shape
Write server route tests for Dio endpoints
Write UI tests for Dio prompt behavior
Run typecheck and test-web/build-all
Explore web provider/model architecture and existing seams

Add Nitro routes for checkout, webhook, claim, recover

32: function modelCost() {

34: 		input: 0,

38: 	};
39: }

41: function toContextWindow(value: unknown, fallback = 0): number {
42: 	if (typeof value === "number" && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
43: 	if (typeof value === "string") {
44: 		const parsed = Number.parseInt(value, 10);
45: 		if (Number.isFinite(parsed)) return parsed;
46: 	}
47: 	return fallback;
48: }

50: function inferInputModes(model: any): Array<"text" | "image"> {
51: 	const supported = new Set<string>();
52: 
53: 	if (Array.isArray(model?.input)) {
54: 		for (const entry of model.input) {
55: 			if (entry === "image" || entry === "text") supported.add(entry);
56: 		}
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	if (Array.isArray(model?.input_modalities)) {
60: 		for (const entry of model.input_modalities) {
61: 			if (entry === "image" || entry === "text") supported.add(entry);
62: 		}
63: 	}
64: 
65: 	if (Array.isArray(model?.modalities)) {
66: 		for (const entry of model.modalities) {
67: 			if (entry === "image" || entry === "text") supported.add(entry);
68: 		}
69: 	}
70: 
71: 	if (model?.vision === true) supported.add("image");
72: 	if (supported.size === 0) supported.add("text");
73: 
74: 	return Array.from(supported) as Array<"text" | "image">;
75: }
76: 
77: function inferReasoning(model: any): boolean {
78: 	if (typeof model?.reasoning === "boolean") return model.reasoning;
79: 	if (typeof model?.supports_reasoning === "boolean") return model.supports_reasoning;
80: 	if (Array.isArray(model?.capabilities)) return model.capabilities.includes("thinking");
81: 	const name = String(model?.id ?? model?.name ?? "").toLowerCase();
82: 	return name.includes("thinking") || name.includes("reasoning");
83: }

98: async function fetchJson(url: string, apiKey?: string, options: { method?: "GET" | "POST"; body?: unknown } = {}): Promise<any> {
99: 	const proxied = proxiedProviderRequestUrl(url);
100: 	const headers: Record<string, string> = {
101: 		"x-target-url": proxied.targetBaseUrl,
102: 	};
103: 
104: 	if (options.body !== undefined) headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json";
105: 	if (apiKey) {
106: 		headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${apiKey}`;
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	const response = await withApiRetry(async () => {
110: 		const result = await fetch(proxied.url, {
111: 			method: options.method ?? "GET",
112: 			headers,
113: 			body: options.body === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(options.body),
114: 		});
115: 		if (!result.ok) {
116: 			const retryAfter = result.headers.get("retry-after");
117: 			throw new Error(`HTTP ${result.status}: ${result.statusText}${retryAfter ? ` retry-after: ${retryAfter}` : ""}`);
118: 		}
119: 		return result;
120: 	});
121: 
122: 	return await response.json();
123: }
124: 
125: async function discoverOpenAiCompatibleModels(
126: 	provider: KeatingCustomProvider,
127: ): Promise<{ apiBaseUrl: string; models: Model<Api>[] }> {
128: 	const baseUrl = trimTrailingSlash(provider.baseUrl);
129: 	const candidateBaseUrls = baseUrl.endsWith("/v1") ? [baseUrl] : [baseUrl, `${baseUrl}/v1`];
130: 
131: 	let lastError: unknown;
132: 	for (const apiBaseUrl of candidateBaseUrls) {
133: 		try {
134: 			const payload = await fetchJson(`${apiBaseUrl}/models`, provider.apiKey);
135: 			const records = Array.isArray(payload?.data) ? payload.data : Array.isArray(payload) ? payload : [];
136: 				const models = records
137: 					.map((record: any): Model<Api> | null => {
138: 					const id = String(record?.id ?? record?.name ?? "").trim();
139: 					if (!id) return null;
140: 
141: 					const contextWindow = toContextWindow(
142: 						record?.context_window ?? record?.context_length ?? record?.max_context_length ?? record?.max_model_len,
143: 					);
144: 					const maxTokens = toContextWindow(record?.max_tokens ?? record?.max_output_tokens, contextWindow || 4096);
145: 
146: 					return {
147: 						id,
148: 						name: String(record?.name ?? id),
149: 						api: manualProviderApi(provider.type),
150: 						provider: provider.name,
151: 						baseUrl: apiBaseUrl,
152: 						reasoning: inferReasoning(record),
153: 						input: inferInputModes(record),
154: 						cost: modelCost(),
155: 						contextWindow,
156: 						maxTokens,
157: 					};
158: 					})
159: 					.filter((model: Model<Api> | null): model is Model<Api> => model !== null);
160: 
161: 			return { apiBaseUrl, models };
162: 		} catch (error) {
163: 			lastError = error;
164: 		}
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	throw lastError instanceof Error ? lastError : new Error(`Failed to discover models for ${provider.name}`);
168: }
169: 
170: async function discoverOllamaModels(baseUrl: string): Promise<Model<Api>[]> {
171: 	const trimmedBaseUrl = trimTrailingSlash(baseUrl);
172: 	const payload = await fetchJson(`${trimmedBaseUrl}/api/tags`);
173: 	const models = Array.isArray(payload?.models) ? payload.models : [];
174: 
175: 	const discovered = await Promise.all(
176: 		models.map(async (model: any): Promise<Model<Api> | null> => {
177: 			const details = await fetchJson(`${trimmedBaseUrl}/api/show`, undefined, {
178: 				method: "POST",
179: 				body: { model: model.name },
180: 			});
181: 			const capabilities: string[] = (details as any).capabilities || [];
182: 			if (!capabilities.includes("tools")) return null;
183: 
184: 			const modelInfo: any = details.model_info || {};
185: 			const architecture = modelInfo["general.architecture"] || "";
186: 			const contextWindow = toContextWindow(modelInfo[`${architecture}.context_length`], 8192);
187: 
188: 			return {
189: 				id: model.name,

274: 				models = await discoverOllamaModels(provider.baseUrl);

276: 			case "llama.cpp":

1: import type { Api, Model } from "@earendil-works/pi-ai";
2: 
3: const PROXY_PATH = "/api/chat-proxy";
4: 
5: const DIRECT_HOST_SUFFIXES = [
6: 	"api.openai.com",
7: 	"generativelanguage.googleapis.com",
8: 	"api.groq.com",
9: 	"api.x.ai",
10: 	"api.mistral.ai",
11: 	"huggingface.co",
12: 	"api.cerebras.ai",
13: 	"amazonaws.com",
14: 	"azure.com",
15: 	"github.com",
16: 	"openrouter.ai",
17: ];
18: 
19: function currentOrigin(): string {
20: 	return globalThis.location?.origin ?? "";
21: }
22: 
23: function hostnameMatches(hostname: string, suffix: string): boolean {
24: 	return hostname === suffix || hostname.endsWith(`.${suffix}`);
25: }
26: 
27: function isLocalHost(hostname: string): boolean {
28: 	return hostname === "localhost" || hostname === "127.0.0.1" || hostname === "::1";
29: }
30: 
31: function parseUrl(value: string): URL | null {
32: 	try {
33: 		return new URL(value, currentOrigin() || "http://localhost");
34: 	} catch {
35: 		return null;
36: 	}
37: }
38: 
39: export function chatProxyBaseUrl(): string {
40: 	const origin = currentOrigin();
41: 	return origin ? `${origin}${PROXY_PATH}` : PROXY_PATH;
42: }
43: 
44: export function proxyTargetHeader(baseUrl: string): string {
45: 	return baseUrl.replace(/\/+$/, "");
46: }
47: 
48: export function shouldProxyModel(model: Model<Api>): boolean {
49: 	if (!model.baseUrl || model.provider === "browser") return false;
50: 	if (model.provider === "anthropic" || model.api === "anthropic-messages") return true;
51: 
52: 	const url = parseUrl(model.baseUrl);
53: 	if (!url) return true;
54: 
55: 	if (url.origin === currentOrigin() && url.pathname.startsWith(PROXY_PATH)) return false;
56: 	if (isLocalHost(url.hostname)) return true;
57: 
58: 	return !DIRECT_HOST_SUFFIXES.some((suffix) => hostnameMatches(url.hostname, suffix));
59: }
60: 
61: export function proxiedProviderRequestUrl(targetUrl: string): { url: string; targetBaseUrl: string } {
62: 	const parsed = new URL(targetUrl);
63: 	return {
64: 		url: `${chatProxyBaseUrl()}${parsed.pathname}${parsed.search}`,
65: 		targetBaseUrl: parsed.origin,
66: 	};
67: }

1: import {

26: }
27: 
28: function hasGoogleSearchTool(payload: any): boolean {
29: 	return Array.isArray(payload?.config?.tools)
30: 		&& payload.config.tools.some((tool: any) => tool?.googleSearch || tool?.google_search);
31: }
32: 
33: export function applyGoogleSearchGrounding(payload: unknown, model: Model<Api>, hasApiKey: boolean): unknown | undefined {
34: 	if (!hasApiKey || loadKeatingUiSettings().googleGrounding === "off" || !isGoogleGroundingModel(model)) {
35: 		return undefined;
36: 	}
37: 
38: 	const params = payload as any;
39: 	const existingTools = Array.isArray(params?.config?.tools) ? params.config.tools : [];
40: 	if (existingTools.length > 0 && !supportsGoogleSearchWithCustomTools(model)) {
41: 		return undefined;

43: 	if (hasGoogleSearchTool(params)) {
44: 		return undefined;
45: 	}
46: 
47: 	return {
48: 		...params,
49: 		config: {
50: 			...(params?.config ?? {}),
51: 			tools: [...existingTools, { googleSearch: {} }],
52: 		},
53: 	};
54: }

56: function mergeOnPayload(
57: 	options: SimpleStreamOptions | undefined,
58: 	model: Model<Api>,
59: ): SimpleStreamOptions | undefined {
60: 	if (model.provider !== "google") return options;
61: 
62: 	return {
63: 		...options,
64: 		onPayload: async (payload, payloadModel) => {
65: 			const userPayload = await options?.onPayload?.(payload, payloadModel);
66: 			const nextPayload = userPayload ?? payload;
67: 			const groundedPayload = applyGoogleSearchGrounding(nextPayload, payloadModel, !!options?.apiKey);
68: 			return groundedPayload ?? userPayload;
69: 		},
70: 	};
71: }

73: function createBrowserStreamFn() {
74: 	return async (_model: Model<Api>, context: Context, options?: SimpleStreamOptions) => {
75: 		const stream = createAssistantMessageEventStream();
76: 		const abortSignal = options?.signal;
77: 
78: 		const defaultFields = {
79: 			api: "browser" as const,
80: 			provider: "browser" as const,
81: 			model: getModelId(),
82: 			usage: {
83: 				input: 0,
84: 				output: 0,
85: 				cacheRead: 0,
86: 				cacheWrite: 0,
87: 				totalTokens: 0,
88: 				cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0, total: 0 },
89: 			},
90: 		};
91: 
92: 		(async () => {
93: 			try {
94: 				if (abortSignal?.aborted) {
95: 					stream.end({
96: 						...defaultFields,
97: 						role: "assistant",
98: 						content: [],
99: 						stopReason: "aborted",
100: 						errorMessage: "Request aborted",
101: 						timestamp: Date.now(),
102: 					} as AssistantMessage);
103: 					return;
104: 				}
105: 
106: 				const userMessages = context.messages
107: 					.filter((m): m is Extract<typeof m, { role: "user" }> => m.role === "user")
108: 					.map((m) => {
109: 						const content = m.content;
110: 						if (typeof content === "string") return content;
111: 						return content
112: 							.filter((c): c is Extract<typeof c, { type: "text" }> => c.type === "text")
113: 							.map((c) => c.text)
114: 							.join("\n");
115: 					});
116: 
117: 				const systemPrompt = context.systemPrompt || "";
118: 				const conversationHistory = userMessages.join("\n\n");
119: 				const fullPrompt = systemPrompt
120: 					? `${systemPrompt}\n\n${conversationHistory}`
121: 					: conversationHistory;
122: 
123: 				const partialMessage: AssistantMessage = {
124: 					...defaultFields,
125: 					role: "assistant",
126: 					content: [{ type: "text", text: "" }],
127: 					stopReason: "stop",
128: 					timestamp: Date.now(),
129: 				};
130: 
131: 				stream.push({ type: "start", partial: partialMessage });
132: 				stream.push({ type: "text_start", contentIndex: 0, partial: partialMessage });
133: 
134: 				const response = await localModel.generate(
135: 					fullPrompt,
136: 					{ max_length: options?.maxTokens ?? 1024, temperature: options?.temperature ?? 0.7 },
137: 					(token: string) => {
138: 						const textBlock = partialMessage.content[0];
139: 						if (textBlock.type === "text") textBlock.text += token;
140: 						stream.push({ type: "text_delta", contentIndex: 0, delta: token, partial: partialMessage });
141: 					},
142: 				);
143: 
144: 				if (abortSignal?.aborted) {
145: 					stream.end({
146: 						...defaultFields,
147: 						role: "assistant",
148: 						content: [{ type: "text", text: response }],
149: 						stopReason: "aborted",
150: 						errorMessage: "Request aborted",
151: 						timestamp: Date.now(),
152: 					} as AssistantMessage);
153: 					return;
154: 				}
155: 
156: 				stream.push({
157: 					type: "text_end",
158: 					contentIndex: 0,
159: 					content: response,
160: 					partial: partialMessage,
161: 				});
162: 				stream.end({
163: 					...defaultFields,
164: 					role: "assistant",
165: 					content: [{ type: "text", text: response }],
166: 					stopReason: "stop",
167: 					timestamp: Date.now(),
168: 				} as AssistantMessage);
169: 			} catch (error) {
170: 				stream.end({
171: 					...defaultFields,
172: 					role: "assistant",
173: 					content: [],
174: 					stopReason: "error",
175: 					errorMessage: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
176: 					timestamp: Date.now(),
177: 				} as AssistantMessage);
178: 			}
179: 		})();
180: 
181: 		return stream;
182: 	};
183: }

185: export async function hybridStreamFn(model: Model<Api>, context: Context, options?: SimpleStreamOptions) {
186: 	if (model.provider === "browser") {
187: 		return normalizeToolCallStream(await createBrowserStreamFn()(model, context, options), context);
188: 	}
189: 
190: 	const apiKey = options?.apiKey ?? await getProviderApiKey(model.provider);
191: 	const streamOptions: SimpleStreamOptions | undefined = apiKey ? { ...options, apiKey } : options;
192: 
193: 	if (shouldProxyModel(model)) {
194: 		const proxiedModel = {
195: 			...model,
196: 			baseUrl: chatProxyBaseUrl(),
197: 		};
198: 		const proxiedOptions: SimpleStreamOptions = {
199: 			...streamOptions,
200: 			headers: {
201: 				...streamOptions?.headers,
202: 				"x-target-url": proxyTargetHeader(model.baseUrl),
203: 			},
204: 		};
205: 		if (import.meta.env.DEV) {
206: 			const hasApiKey = !!proxiedOptions.apiKey;
207: 			console.log(`[keating:stream] proxy ${model.provider} -> ${model.baseUrl} (apiKey=${hasApiKey})`);
208: 		}
209: 		const mergedOptions = mergeOnPayload(proxiedOptions, proxiedModel);
210: 		return normalizeToolCallStream(
211: 			streamWithApiRetry(proxiedModel, context, mergedOptions, (nextOptions) => streamSimple(proxiedModel, context, nextOptions)),
212: 			context,
213: 		);
214: 	}
215: 
216: 	const mergedOptions = mergeOnPayload(streamOptions, model);
217: 	return normalizeToolCallStream(
218: 		streamWithApiRetry(model, context, mergedOptions, (nextOptions) => streamSimple(model, context, nextOptions)),
219: 		context,
220: 	);
221: }

1: import { defineConfig, Plugin } from 'vite';
2: import { resolve } from 'path';
3: import { VitePWA } from 'vite-plugin-pwa';
4: import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react';
5: import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite';
6: import nodepod from '@scelar/nodepod/vite';
7: import * as https from 'https';
8: import * as http from 'http';
9: import { copyFileSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync, statSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs';
10: import { join, dirname } from 'node:path';
11: 
12: type AgentRuntimeMode = 'browser-only' | 'remote' | 'cloud';
13: 
14: /**
15:  * Copy `node_modules/manim-web/dist/` into the build's public dir as
16:  * `/manim-web/...` so the animate tool can author raw manim-web scenes
17:  * and render them in an iframe that imports the library at this stable
18:  * public path. Works in both dev (Vite serves /manim-web/) and prod
19:  * (Nitro bundles the public/ dir).
20:  */
21: function manimWebPublicPlugin(): Plugin {
22: 	const SOURCE = resolve(__dirname, '../node_modules/manim-web/dist');
23: 	let publicDir: string | null = null;
24: 	const ensureCopied = (): boolean => {
25: 		if (!publicDir) return false;
26: 		const target = join(publicDir, 'manim-web');
27: 		if (existsSync(target)) return true;
28: 		try {
29: 			mkdirSync(target, { recursive: true });
30: 		} catch {
31: 			return false;
32: 		}
33: 		return true;
34: 	};
35: 	const copyRecursive = (src: string, dst: string): void => {
36: 		if (!existsSync(src)) return;
37: 		for (const entry of readdirSync(src)) {
38: 			const sourcePath = join(src, entry);
39: 			const targetPath = join(dst, entry);
40: 			const stat = statSync(sourcePath);
41: 			if (stat.isDirectory()) {
42: 				mkdirSync(targetPath, { recursive: true });
43: 				copyRecursive(sourcePath, targetPath);
44: 			} else {
45: 				mkdirSync(dirname(targetPath), { recursive: true });
46: 				copyFileSync(sourcePath, targetPath);
47: 			}
48: 		}
49: 	};
50: 	return {
51: 		name: 'keating-manim-web-public',
52: 		apply: () => true,
53: 		configResolved(config) {
54: 			publicDir = config.publicDir;
55: 			if (publicDir) {
56: 				const target = join(publicDir, 'manim-web');
57: 				if (!existsSync(target)) {
58: 					try {
59: 						mkdirSync(target, { recursive: true });
60: 						copyRecursive(SOURCE, target);
61: 						// eslint-disable-next-line no-console
62: 						console.log(`[manim-web] copied ${SOURCE} -> ${target}`);
63: 					} catch (e) {
64: 						// eslint-disable-next-line no-console
65: 						console.error(`[manim-web] copy failed:`, e);
66: 					}
67: 				}
68: 			}
69: 		},
70: 		configureServer(server) {
71: 			if (!server.config.publicDir) return;
72: 			const target = join(server.config.publicDir, 'manim-web');
73: 			if (existsSync(target)) return;
74: 			mkdirSync(target, { recursive: true });
75: 			copyRecursive(SOURCE, target);
76: 		},
77: 	};
78: }
79: 
80: function env(name: string): string | null {
81:   const value = process.env[name]?.trim();
82:   return value ? value : null;
83: }
84: 
85: function agentRuntimeMode(): AgentRuntimeMode {
86:   const mode = env('KEATING_WEB_AGENT_MODE');
87:   return mode === 'remote' || mode === 'cloud' ? mode : 'browser-only';
88: }
89: 
90: function agentRuntimeTargetBase(mode: AgentRuntimeMode): string | null {
91:   if (mode === 'browser-only') return null;
92:   if (mode === 'remote') return env('KEATING_WEB_REMOTE_ENDPOINT');
93:   return env('KEATING_WEB_CLOUD_ENDPOINT') || 'https://keating.help';
94: }
95: 
96: function chatProxyPlugin(): Plugin {
97:   return {
98:     name: 'chat-proxy',
99:     configureServer(server) {
100:       server.middlewares.use((req, res, next) => {
101:         if (req.url === '/api/agent-runtime/config') {
102:           const mode = agentRuntimeMode();
103:           const body = JSON.stringify({
104:             mode,
105:             label: mode === 'cloud' ? 'Keating Cloud agent' : mode === 'remote' ? 'Remote microVM agent' : 'Browser-only agent',
106:             executionEndpoint: mode === 'browser-only' ? null : '/api/agent-runtime/remote',
107:             cloudEndpoint: mode === 'cloud' ? (process.env.KEATING_WEB_CLOUD_ENDPOINT || 'https://keating.help') : null,
108:             remote: mode === 'remote'
109:               ? {
110:                   provider: process.env.KEATING_WEB_REMOTE_PROVIDER || 'microsandbox',
111:                   endpoint: process.env.KEATING_WEB_REMOTE_ENDPOINT || null,
112:                   region: process.env.KEATING_WEB_REMOTE_REGION || null,
113:                   snapshot: process.env.KEATING_WEB_REMOTE_SNAPSHOT || null,
114:                   cpu: process.env.KEATING_WEB_REMOTE_CPU || null,
115:                   memory: process.env.KEATING_WEB_REMOTE_MEMORY || null,
116:                   disk: process.env.KEATING_WEB_REMOTE_DISK || null,
117:                 }
118:               : null,
119:             capabilities: {
120:               browserLocal: true,
121:               remoteSandbox: mode !== 'browser-only',
122:               secureIsolation: mode !== 'browser-only',
123:               nativeBinaries: mode !== 'browser-only',
124:               serverBrokeredSecrets: mode !== 'browser-only',
125:               durableCompute: mode !== 'browser-only',
126:             },
127:             fallback: {
128:               localFirst: true,
129:               remoteAvailable: mode !== 'browser-only',
130:               message: mode === 'browser-only'
131:                 ? 'Run supported agent work in the browser. Surface a fallback error for secure isolation, native binaries, brokered secrets, durable compute, or public inbound networking.'
132:                 : 'Run browser-compatible work locally first. Route remote-only work through the configured backend.',
133:             },
134:           });
135:           res.setHeader('content-type', 'application/json');
136:           res.end(body);
137:           return;
138:         }
139: 
140:         if (req.url?.startsWith('/api/agent-runtime/remote')) {
141:           const mode = agentRuntimeMode();
142:           const targetBase = agentRuntimeTargetBase(mode);
143:           if (!targetBase) {
144:             res.statusCode = mode === 'browser-only' ? 403 : 503;
145:             res.setHeader('content-type', 'application/json');
146:             res.end(JSON.stringify({
147:               error: mode === 'browser-only'
148:                 ? 'Remote agent runtime is disabled in browser-only mode.'
149:                 : 'Remote agent runtime endpoint is not configured.',
150:             }));
151:             return;
152:           }
153: 
154:           const proxyPath = req.url.replace(/^\/api\/agent-runtime\/remote\/?/, '');
155:           const targetUrl = new URL(`${targetBase.replace(/\/$/, '')}/api/agent-runtime/${proxyPath}`);
156:           const forbiddenHeaders = ['origin', 'host', 'referer'];
157:           const outHeaders: Record<string, string> = {};
158:           for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(req.headers)) {
159:             if (value && !forbiddenHeaders.includes(key.toLowerCase())) {
160:               outHeaders[key] = Array.isArray(value) ? value.join(', ') : value;
161:             }
162:           }
163:           outHeaders.host = targetUrl.host;
164:           outHeaders['x-keating-agent-runtime-mode'] = mode;
165: 
166:           const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
167:           req.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => chunks.push(chunk));
168:           req.on('end', () => {
169:             const body = Buffer.concat(chunks);
170:             const isHttps = targetUrl.protocol === 'https:';
171:             const lib = isHttps ? https : http;
172:             const proxyReq = lib.request(
173:               {
174:                 hostname: targetUrl.hostname,
175:                 port: targetUrl.port || (isHttps ? 443 : 80),
176:                 path: targetUrl.pathname + targetUrl.search,
177:                 method: req.method,
178:                 headers: outHeaders,
179:               },
180:               (proxyRes) => {
181:                 const resHeaders = { ...proxyRes.headers } as Record<string, string>;
182:                 delete resHeaders['transfer-encoding'];
183:                 res.writeHead(proxyRes.statusCode!, resHeaders);
184:                 proxyRes.pipe(res);
185:               },
186:             );
187:             proxyReq.on('error', (err) => {
188:               console.error('[agent-runtime-proxy] request error:', err.message);
189:               if (!res.headersSent) {
190:                 res.statusCode = 502;
191:                 res.end('Agent runtime proxy error: ' + err.message);
192:               }
193:             });
194:             proxyReq.write(body);
195:             proxyReq.end();
196:           });
197:           return;
198:         }
199: 
200:         if (!req.url?.startsWith('/api/chat-proxy')) return next();
201: 
202:         const targetBaseUrl = req.headers['x-target-url'] as string;
203:         if (!targetBaseUrl) {
204:           res.statusCode = 400;
205:           res.end('Missing x-target-url header');
206:           return;
207:         }
208: 
209:         let parsedTarget: URL;
210:         try {
211:           parsedTarget = new URL(targetBaseUrl);
212:         } catch {
213:           res.statusCode = 400;
214:           res.end('Invalid x-target-url header');
215:           return;
216:         }
217: 
218:         const proxyPath = req.url.replace(/^\/api\/chat-proxy\/?/, '');
219:         const fullTarget = `${parsedTarget.href.replace(/\/$/, '')}/${proxyPath}`;
220:         const targetUrl = new URL(fullTarget);
221: 
222:         const forbiddenHeaders = [
223:           'x-stainless-os', 'x-stainless-lang', 'x-stainless-package-version',
224:           'x-stainless-runtime', 'x-stainless-runtime-version',
225:           'x-stainless-arch', 'x-stainless-os-version',
226:           'origin', 'host', 'referer', 'x-target-url',
227:         ];
228: 
229:         const outHeaders: Record<string, string> = {};
230:         for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(req.headers)) {
231:           if (value && !forbiddenHeaders.includes(key.toLowerCase())) {
232:             outHeaders[key] = Array.isArray(value) ? value.join(', ') : value;
233:           }
234:         }
235:         outHeaders['host'] = targetUrl.host;
236: 
237:         const hasAuth = 'authorization' in outHeaders;
238:         const hasApiKey = 'x-api-key' in outHeaders;
239:         console.log(`[chat-proxy] ${req.method} ${proxyPath} -> ${targetUrl.hostname} (auth=${hasAuth}, xApiKey=${hasApiKey})`);
240: 
241:         const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
242:         req.on('data', (chunk: Buffer) => chunks.push(chunk));
243:         req.on('end', () => {
244:           const body = Buffer.concat(chunks);
245:           const isHttps = targetUrl.protocol === 'https:';
246:           const lib = isHttps ? https : http;
247:           const proxyReq = lib.request(
248:             {
249:               hostname: targetUrl.hostname,
250:               port: targetUrl.port || (isHttps ? 443 : 80),
251:               path: targetUrl.pathname + targetUrl.search,
252:               method: req.method,
253:               headers: outHeaders,
254:             },
255:             (proxyRes) => {
256:               const resHeaders = { ...proxyRes.headers } as Record<string, string>;
257:               delete resHeaders['transfer-encoding'];
258:               res.writeHead(proxyRes.statusCode!, resHeaders);
259:               proxyRes.pipe(res);
260:             },
261:           );
262:           proxyReq.on('error', (err) => {
263:             console.error('[chat-proxy] request error:', err.message);
264:             if (!res.headersSent) {
265:               res.statusCode = 502;
266:               res.end('Proxy error: ' + err.message);
267:             }
268:           });
269:           proxyReq.write(body);
270:           proxyReq.end();
271:         });
272:       });
273:     },
274:   };
275: }

1: import { defineEventHandler, proxyRequest, getHeader, getRequestURL, getHeaders, createError } from "h3";
2: 
3: export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
4:   const targetBaseUrl = getHeader(event, "x-target-url");
5: 
6:   if (!targetBaseUrl) {
7:     throw createError({
8:       statusCode: 400,
9:       statusMessage: "Missing x-target-url header",
10:     });
11:   }
12: 
13:   const reqUrl = getRequestURL(event);
14:   const proxyPath = reqUrl.pathname.replace(/^\/api\/chat-proxy\/?/, "") + reqUrl.search;
15:   const fullTargetUrl = `${targetBaseUrl.replace(/\/$/, "")}/${proxyPath}`;
16: 
17:   const forbiddenHeaders = [
18:     "x-stainless-os",
19:     "x-stainless-lang",
20:     "x-stainless-package-version",
21:     "x-stainless-runtime",
22:     "x-stainless-runtime-version",
23:     "x-stainless-arch",
24:     "x-stainless-os-version",
25:     "origin",
26:     "host",
27:     "referer",
28:     "x-target-url",
29:   ];
30: 
31:   const headers: Record<string, string> = { ...getHeaders(event) };
32:   for (const forbidden of forbiddenHeaders) {
33:     delete headers[forbidden];
34:   }
35: 
36:   if (process.dev || import.meta.env?.DEV) {
37:     const hasAuth = !!headers["authorization"];
38:     const hasApiKey = !!headers["x-api-key"];
39:     console.log(`[chat-proxy] ${event.method} ${proxyPath} -> ${targetBaseUrl} (auth=${hasAuth}, xApiKey=${hasApiKey})`);
40:   }
41: 
42:   return proxyRequest(event, fullTargetUrl, {
43:     headers,
44:   });
45: });

(End of file - total 45 lines)
</content>

1: import { describe, expect, it } from "bun:test";
2: 
3: import {
4: 	applyReview,
5: 	formatDueIn,
6: 	formatInterval,
7: 	getDeckStats,
8: 	getDueCards,
9: 	initialSrsState,
10: 	isDue,
11: 	MIN_EASE,
12: 	scheduleDueCards,
13: 	validateDeckDraft,
14: 	type Flashcard,
15: 	type FlashcardDeck,
16: } from "../keating/srs";
17: 
18: function makeCard(overrides: Partial<Flashcard> = {}): Flashcard {
19: 	const now = overrides.createdAt ?? Date.now();
20: 	return {
21: 		id: overrides.id ?? `card-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`,
22: 		front: overrides.front ?? "Front",
23: 		back: overrides.back ?? "Back",
24: 		srs: overrides.srs ?? initialSrsState(now),
25: 		createdAt: now,
26: 		updatedAt: now,
27: 		...(overrides.tags ? { tags: overrides.tags } : {}),
28: 	};
29: }
30: 
31: function makeDeck(cards: Flashcard[]): FlashcardDeck {
32: 	const now = Date.now();
33: 	return {
34: 		id: "deck-1",
35: 		topic: "Test topic",
36: 		slug: "test-topic",
37: 		title: "Test deck",
38: 		cards,
39: 		createdAt: now,
40: 		updatedAt: now,
41: 	};
42: }
43: 
44: describe("SM-2 flashcard SRS", () => {
45: 	it("applies the first successful review as a 1-day interval", () => {
46: 		const now = 1_000_000;
47: 		const srs = initialSrsState(now);
48: 		const outcome = applyReview(srs, 2, now + 60_000);
49: 		expect(outcome.appliedIntervalDays).toBe(1);
50: 		expect(outcome.isLapse).toBe(false);
51: 		expect(outcome.next.reps).toBe(1);
52: 		expect(outcome.next.ease).toBe(2.5);
53: 		expect(outcome.next.dueAt).toBe(now + 60_000 + 86_400_000);
54: 	});
55: 
56: 	it("applies the second successful review as a 6-day interval", () => {
57: 		const now = 0;
58: 		let srs = initialSrsState(now);
59: 		srs = applyReview(srs, 2, now).next;
60: 		const second = applyReview(srs, 2, now + 86_400_000);
61: 		expect(second.appliedIntervalDays).toBe(6);
62: 		expect(second.next.reps).toBe(2);
63: 	});
64: 
65: 	it("scales intervals past the second review by the ease factor", () => {
66: 		const now = 0;
67: 		let srs = initialSrsState(now);
68: 		srs = applyReview(srs, 2, now).next; // 1d
69: 		srs = applyReview(srs, 2, now).next; // 6d
70: 		const third = applyReview(srs, 2, now);
71: 		// 6 * 2.5 = 15
72: 		expect(third.appliedIntervalDays).toBe(15);
73: 		expect(third.next.reps).toBe(3);
74: 	});
75: 
76: 	it("shortens interval on Hard and lengthens on Easy", () => {
77: 		const now = 0;
78: 		let srs = initialSrsState(now);
79: 		srs = applyReview(srs, 2, now).next; // 1d
80: 		srs = applyReview(srs, 2, now).next; // 6d
81: 		const good = applyReview(srs, 2, now).appliedIntervalDays; // 15
82: 		const hard = applyReview(srs, 1, now).appliedIntervalDays;
83: 		const easy = applyReview(srs, 3, now).appliedIntervalDays;
84: 		expect(hard).toBeLessThan(good);
85: 		expect(easy).toBeGreaterThan(good);
86: 	});
87: 
88: 	it("lapses on Again: schedules 10 min later, drops ease, resets reps", () => {
89: 		const now = 0;
90: 		let srs = initialSrsState(now);
91: 		srs = applyReview(srs, 2, now).next;
92: 		srs = applyReview(srs, 2, now).next;
93: 		srs = applyReview(srs, 2, now).next;
94: 		const beforeEase = srs.ease;
95: 		const beforeReps = srs.reps;
96: 		const lapse = applyReview(srs, 0, now);
97: 		expect(lapse.isLapse).toBe(true);
98: 		expect(lapse.appliedIntervalDays).toBe(0);
99: 		expect(lapse.next.reps).toBe(0);
100: 		expect(lapse.next.lapses).toBe(1);
101: 		expect(lapse.next.ease).toBeLessThan(beforeEase);
102: 		expect(lapse.next.dueAt).toBe(now + 10 * 60 * 1000);
103: 		expect(beforeReps).toBe(3);
104: 	});
105: 
106: 	it("clamps ease to the SM-2 minimum", () => {
107: 		let srs = initialSrsState(0);
108: 		for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
109: 			srs = applyReview(srs, 1, 0).next;
110: 		}
111: 		expect(srs.ease).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(MIN_EASE);
112: 	});
113: });
114: 
115: describe("flashcard scheduling helpers", () => {
116: 	it("isDue respects the card's dueAt", () => {
117: 		const now = 1000;
118: 		const fresh = initialSrsState(now);
119: 		expect(isDue(fresh, now)).toBe(true);
120: 		expect(isDue({ ...fresh, dueAt: now + 10_000 }, now)).toBe(false);
121: 	});
122: 
123: 	it("getDueCards returns only cards whose SRS is due", () => {
124: 		const now = 100_000;
125: 		const deck = makeDeck([
126: 			makeCard({ id: "due", srs: { ...initialSrsState(now), dueAt: now - 1 } }),
127: 			makeCard({ id: "fresh", srs: { ...initialSrsState(now), dueAt: now + 100_000 } }),
128: 		]);
129: 		const due = getDueCards(deck, now);
130: 		expect(due.map((c) => c.id)).toEqual(["due"]);
131: 	});
132: 
133: 	it("scheduleDueCards surfaces lapses first, then oldest-due", () => {
134: 		const now = 1000;
135: 		const deck = makeDeck([
136: 			makeCard({ id: "old", srs: { ...initialSrsState(now), dueAt: now - 100, lapses: 0, reps: 2 } }),
137: 			makeCard({ id: "lapse", srs: { ...initialSrsState(now), dueAt: now - 50, lapses: 2, reps: 4 } }),
138: 			makeCard({ id: "newest", srs: { ...initialSrsState(now), dueAt: now - 10, lapses: 0, reps: 1 } }),
139: 		]);
140: 		const ordered = scheduleDueCards(getDueCards(deck, now), now).map((c) => c.id);
141: 		expect(ordered[0]).toBe("lapse");
142: 		// The non-lapsing cards are ordered by dueAt ascending.
143: 		expect(ordered.slice(1)).toEqual(["old", "newest"]);
144: 	});
145: 
146: 	it("getDeckStats classifies cards into fresh/learning/review/mature", () => {
147: 		const now = 0;
148: 		const deck = makeDeck([
149: 			makeCard({ id: "fresh", srs: { ...initialSrsState(now), reps: 0 } }),
150: 			makeCard({ id: "learning", srs: { ...initialSrsState(now), reps: 3, intervalDays: 10 } }),
151: 			makeCard({ id: "review", srs: { ...initialSrsState(now), reps: 5, intervalDays: 45 } }),
152: 			makeCard({ id: "mature", srs: { ...initialSrsState(now), reps: 8, intervalDays: 120 } }),
153: 		]);
154: 		const stats = getDeckStats(deck, now);
155: 		expect(stats).toEqual({ total: 4, dueNow: 4, fresh: 1, learning: 1, review: 1, mature: 1 });
156: 	});
157: });
158: 
159: describe("format helpers", () => {
160: 	it("formatInterval produces human-readable labels", () => {
161: 		expect(formatInterval(0)).toBe("10m");
162: 		expect(formatInterval(0.01)).toMatch(/m/);
163: 		expect(formatInterval(1)).toBe("1d");
164: 		expect(formatInterval(3)).toBe("3d");
165: 		expect(formatInterval(30)).toBe("1mo");
166: 		expect(formatInterval(365)).toBe("1.0y");
167: 	});
168: 
169: 	it("formatDueIn handles future, now, and overdue", () => {
170: 		const now = 1_000_000;
171: 		expect(formatDueIn(now, now)).toBe("now");
172: 		expect(formatDueIn(now + 3 * 86_400_000, now)).toBe("in 3d");
173: 		expect(formatDueIn(now - 2 * 86_400_000, now)).toBe("2d overdue");
174: 	});
175: });
176: 
177: describe("validateDeckDraft", () => {
178: 	it("rejects non-array input", () => {
179: 		const result = validateDeckDraft(null);
180: 		expect(result.ok).toBe(false);
181: 		if (!result.ok) expect(result.error).toMatch(/array/);
182: 	});
183: 
184: 	it("rejects too few cards", () => {
185: 		const result = validateDeckDraft([{ front: "Q?", back: "A." }]);
186: 		expect(result.ok).toBe(false);
187: 		if (!result.ok) expect(result.error).toMatch(/at least 2/);
188: 	});
189: 
190: 	it("rejects too many cards", () => {
191: 		const tooMany = Array.from({ length: 250 }, (_, i) => ({ front: `Q${i}?`, back: `A${i}.` }));
192: 		const result = validateDeckDraft(tooMany);
193: 		expect(result.ok).toBe(false);
194: 		if (!result.ok) expect(result.error).toMatch(/Too many/);
195: 	});
196: 
197: 	it("rejects short fronts or backs", () => {
198: 		const result = validateDeckDraft([
199: 			{ front: "ab", back: "answer here" },
200: 			{ front: "Valid question?", back: "Valid answer." },
201: 		]);
202: 		expect(result.ok).toBe(false);
203: 		if (!result.ok) expect(result.error).toMatch(/front is too short/);
204: 	});
205: 
206: 	it("rejects duplicate fronts", () => {
207: 		const result = validateDeckDraft([
208: 			{ front: "Same question?", back: "First answer." },
209: 			{ front: "same question?", back: "Second answer." },
210: 		]);
211: 		expect(result.ok).toBe(false);
212: 		if (!result.ok) expect(result.error).toMatch(/Duplicate/);
213: 	});
214: 
215: 	it("accepts well-formed cards and preserves tags", () => {
216: 		const result = validateDeckDraft([
217: 			{ front: "What is recursion?", back: "A function that calls itself." },
218: 			{ front: "What is a closure?", back: "A function with its lexical environment.", tags: ["js", "fundamentals"] },
219: 		]);
220: 		expect(result.ok).toBe(true);
221: 		if (result.ok) {
222: 			expect(result.cards).toHaveLength(2);
223: 			expect(result.cards?.[1].tags).toEqual(["js", "fundamentals"]);
224: 		}
225: 	});
226: 
227: 	it("filters out non-string tags silently", () => {
228: 		const result = validateDeckDraft([
229: 			{ front: "Q1?", back: "A1." },

231: 		]);
232: 		expect(result.ok).toBe(true);

234: 			expect(result.cards?.[1].tags).toEqual(["valid", "also-valid"]);
235: 		}
236: 	});
237: });

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { createError, defineEventHandler, readBody } from "h3";
2: import { useStorage } from "nitro/storage";
3: 
4: const MAX_SHARE_BYTES = 512 * 1024;
5: const SHARE_ID_BYTES = 9;
6: const SHARE_ID_PATTERN = /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,32}$/;
7: 
8: function compactShareId() {
9: 	const bytes = new Uint8Array(SHARE_ID_BYTES);
10: 	globalThis.crypto.getRandomValues(bytes);
11: 	let binary = "";
12: 	for (const byte of bytes) binary += String.fromCharCode(byte);
13: 	return btoa(binary).replace(/\+/g, "-").replace(/\//g, "_").replace(/=+$/g, "");
14: }
15: 
16: function validateSharedSession(value: any) {
17: 	if (!value || typeof value !== "object") {
18: 		throw createError({ statusCode: 400, statusMessage: "Expected shared session object" });
19: 	}
20: 	if (typeof value.title !== "string" || !value.title.trim()) {
21: 		throw createError({ statusCode: 400, statusMessage: "Missing shared session title" });
22: 	}
23: 	if (!Array.isArray(value.messages) || value.messages.length === 0) {
24: 		throw createError({ statusCode: 400, statusMessage: "Missing shared session messages" });
25: 	}
26: 	if (value.id !== undefined && (typeof value.id !== "string" || !SHARE_ID_PATTERN.test(value.id))) {
27: 		throw createError({ statusCode: 400, statusMessage: "Invalid shared session id" });
28: 	}
29: 	for (const message of value.messages) {
30: 		const role = message?.role;
31: 		if (role !== "user" && role !== "user-with-attachments" && role !== "assistant") {
32: 			throw createError({ statusCode: 400, statusMessage: "Invalid shared session message role" });
33: 		}
34: 		if (!Array.isArray(message?.content)) {
35: 			throw createError({ statusCode: 400, statusMessage: "Invalid shared session message content" });
36: 		}
37: 	}
38: }
39: 
40: export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
41: 	if (event.method !== "POST") {
42: 		throw createError({ statusCode: 405, statusMessage: "Method not allowed" });
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	const body = await readBody(event);
46: 	validateSharedSession(body);
47: 
48: 	const size = new TextEncoder().encode(JSON.stringify(body)).length;
49: 	if (size > MAX_SHARE_BYTES) {
50: 		throw createError({ statusCode: 413, statusMessage: "Shared session is too large" });
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	const storage = useStorage("keating:share");
54: 	let id = compactShareId();
55: 	for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 4 && await storage.hasItem(id); attempt++) {
56: 		id = compactShareId();
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	const shared = {
60: 		...body,
61: 		id,
62: 		schemaVersion: 2,
63: 		messageCount: body.messages.length,
64: 	};
65: 	await storage.setItem(id, shared);
66: 	return { id };
67: });

(End of file - total 67 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { defineEventHandler, readBody, createError } from "h3";
2: import { getOAuthServerConfigs, type OAuthServerProviderId } from "./config";
3: 
4: interface TokenRequestBody {
5: 	provider: string;
6: 	code: string;
7: 	redirect_uri: string;
8: 	code_verifier: string;
9: 	state?: string;
10: }
11: 
12: export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
13: 	const body = await readBody<TokenRequestBody>(event);
14: 
15: 	if (!body?.provider || !body?.code || !body?.redirect_uri || !body?.code_verifier) {
16: 		throw createError({
17: 			statusCode: 400,
18: 			statusMessage: "Missing required fields: provider, code, redirect_uri, code_verifier",
19: 		});
20: 	}
21: 
22: 	const configs = getOAuthServerConfigs();
23: 	const config = configs[body.provider as OAuthServerProviderId];
24: 	if (!config) {
25: 		throw createError({
26: 			statusCode: 400,
27: 			statusMessage: `Unsupported OAuth provider: ${body.provider}`,
28: 		});
29: 	}
30: 
31: 	const tokenParams: Record<string, string> = {
32: 		grant_type: "authorization_code",
33: 		code: body.code,
34: 		redirect_uri: body.redirect_uri,
35: 		code_verifier: body.code_verifier,
36: 		client_id: config.clientId,
37: 	};
38: 
39: 	if (body.provider === "anthropic" && body.state) {
40: 		tokenParams.state = body.state;
41: 	}
42: 
43: 	if (config.clientSecret) {
44: 		tokenParams.client_secret = config.clientSecret;
45: 	}
46: 
47: 	try {
48: 		const isJsonTokenProvider = body.provider === "anthropic";
49: 		const response = await fetch(config.tokenUrl, {
50: 			method: "POST",
51: 			headers: { "Content-Type": isJsonTokenProvider ? "application/json" : "application/x-www-form-urlencoded" },
52: 			body: isJsonTokenProvider ? JSON.stringify(tokenParams) : new URLSearchParams(tokenParams).toString(),
53: 		});
54: 
55: 		if (!response.ok) {
56: 			const errorText = await response.text();
57: 			console.error(`[oauth/token] ${body.provider} token exchange failed: ${response.status} ${errorText}`);
58: 			throw createError({
59: 				statusCode: 502,
60: 				statusMessage: `Upstream token exchange failed: ${response.status}`,
61: 			});
62: 		}
63: 
64: 		const tokenData = await response.json();
65: 		return tokenData;
66: 	} catch (error) {
67: 		if ((error as any).statusCode) throw error;
68: 		console.error(`[oauth/token] Error exchanging token for ${body.provider}:`, error);
69: 		throw createError({
70: 			statusCode: 500,
71: 			statusMessage: "Token exchange request failed",
72: 		});
73: 	}
74: });

(End of file - total 74 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# Keating task runner — use `just <task>` to run any task
# All tasks run from the project root unless noted

# Default: list available tasks
default:
    @just --list

# Install dependencies (root + web)
install:
    bun install
    cd web && bun install

# Sync version numbers across all manifests and source files
sync-version:
    bun scripts/sync-version.ts

# Verify that all version strings are in sync (CI-friendly)
check-version:
    bun scripts/sync-version.ts --check

display.text:
# Keating task runner — use `just <task>` to run any task
# All tasks run from the project root unless noted

# Default: list available tasks
default:
    @just --list

# Install dependencies (root + web)
install:
    bun install
    cd web && bun install

# Sync version numbers across all manifests and source files
sync-version:
    bun scripts/sync-version.ts

# Verify that all version strings are in sync (CI-friendly)
check-version:
    bun scripts/sync-version.ts --check

# Build the root TypeScript project
build: check-version
    bun x tsc -p tsconfig.json
    bun scripts/copy-core-templates.ts

# Generate NodePod boot files from source tree
generate-nodepod-boot:
    bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts

# Build everything (root + web)
build-all: sync-version build generate-nodepod-boot
    cd web && bun run build

# Run the root test suite
test:
    bun test ./test/*.test.ts

# Run the web test suite
test-web:
    cd web && bun test

# Run mutation testing
mutate:
    stryker run

# Launch the hyperteacher shell
shell:
    bun src/cli/main.ts shell

# Run the hyperteacher doctor
doctor:
    bun src/cli/main.ts doctor

# Run benchmarks
bench topic="":
    bun src/cli/main.ts bench {{ topic }}

# Evolve the teaching policy
evolve topic="":
    bun src/cli/main.ts evolve {{ topic }}

# Evolve a prompt template
prompt-evolve name="learn":
    bun src/cli/main.ts prompt-evolve {{ name }}

# Generate a lesson plan
plan topic:
    bun src/cli/main.ts plan {{ topic }}

# Generate a lesson map
map topic:
    bun src/cli/main.ts map {{ topic }}

# Generate a fact-checking checklist before teaching
verify topic:
    bun src/cli/main.ts verify {{ topic }}

# Animate a teaching artifact
animate topic:
    bun src/cli/main.ts animate {{ topic }}

# Trace a teaching session
trace substring="":
    bun src/cli/main.ts trace {{ substring }}

# Start the Keating web UI dev server
web:
    cd web && bun run dev

# Build the Keating web UI for production
web-build:
    cd web && bun run build

# Preview the Keating web UI production build
web-preview:
    cd web && bun run build && bun run preview

# Render documentation diagrams
docs-diagrams:
    bun scripts/render-docs-diagrams.mjs

# Render the narrated Keating intro video
video-intro:
    bun scripts/render-keating-intro.mjs

parts.text:
Note: in web/tsconfig.json, "include" does not include the test/ directory or the server/ directory, so tests there won't be typechecked by `tsc --noEmit`. The task says to run `rtk bun run typecheck in web/` after implementation. So I need to ensure my new server files and test files don't break typecheck. Since tsconfig doesn't include them, they shouldn't be checked anyway, but let's be careful.

Let me now understand the @earendil-works/pi-ai types for Model. I should look at the package exports or node_modules.

> ## Documentation Index
> Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://docs.creem.io/llms.txt
> Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.

# Ship Software. Get Paid Globally.

> The most competitive Merchant of Record for SaaS, digital products, and AI tools. Payments, taxes, compliance, affiliates, and revenue splits. All handled.

> 3,000+ teams sell globally with Creem. 3.9% + 40¢ flat. No monthly fees. No tax headaches.

## What is Creem?

Creem is a **Merchant of Record** for developers who sell software globally. Payments, subscriptions, tax compliance, chargebacks, revenue splits, payouts. All handled. You focus on your product.

We are the legal seller of record. We collect and remit VAT, GST, and sales tax in **190+ countries** so you never touch a tax form.

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Most Competitive MoR" icon="money-bill">
    3.9% + 40¢ flat. No monthly fees, no setup costs, no hidden charges. You only pay when you earn.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Global Tax Compliance" icon="globe">
    VAT, GST, and sales tax across 190+ countries. 28+ US states, EU (Estonia OSS), UK, South Korea, and growing.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Built for Developers" icon="code">
    TypeScript SDK, Next.js adapter, CLI, and a clean API. Integrate in minutes.
  </Card>

  <Card title="AI-Native Platform" icon="robot">
    First payment platform with agent onboarding. Your AI assistant can set up your entire store via SKILL.md.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Why Teams Switch to Creem

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Tax Compliance is Eating Your Time" icon="building-columns">
    **The Problem**: Selling globally means VAT, GST, and sales tax across dozens of jurisdictions. Different rates, thresholds, filing requirements. Most founders ignore it (risky), hire expensive accountants, or avoid selling internationally.

    **Creem's Solution**: As your Merchant of Record, we are the legal seller. We calculate, collect, file, and remit all taxes worldwide. You never touch a tax form. This isn't a plugin. It's how Creem works by default.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="You're Paying Too Much in Fees" icon="credit-card">
    **The Problem**: Most MoR solutions charge 5-8% per transaction plus monthly platform fees. On a \$49/mo SaaS, that's \$2-4 per transaction gone.

    **Creem's Solution**: 3.9% + 40¢ flat. No monthly fees. No minimums. No tiered pricing that punishes small volume. The most competitive MoR on the market.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Your Payment Provider Wasn't Built for Developers" icon="terminal">
    **The Problem**: Dashboard-first platforms force you into click-through wizards. That doesn't scale, and it doesn't work for teams that automate everything.

    **Creem's Solution**: Everything is API-first. The CLI lets you manage products, subscriptions, and transactions from the terminal. JSON output for scripting. Interactive TUI for browsing. SKILL.md lets AI agents onboard themselves autonomously.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="You Need More Than Payments" icon="users">
    **The Problem**: Payments are just the start. You need affiliates, revenue splits, license keys, and subscription lifecycle handling. Cobbling together 5 tools is a nightmare.

    **Creem's Solution**: Creem includes affiliate programs, revenue splits, license keys, subscription management (trials, upgrades, downgrades, cancellations), webhooks, and a customer portal. One platform, one integration.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Core Features

<CardGroup cols={3}>
  <Card title="One-time Payments" icon="cart-shopping">
    Sell digital products, templates, courses, or software licenses with instant checkout.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Subscriptions" icon="arrows-rotate">
    Recurring billing with automatic renewals, dunning, trials, and plan management.
  </Card>

  <Card title="License Keys" icon="key">
    Generate and deliver software license keys automatically on purchase.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Affiliate Programs" icon="handshake">
    Built-in affiliate tracking with automatic commission splits and payouts.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Revenue Splits" icon="divide">
    Split payments between co-founders, partners, or collaborators automatically.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Global Tax Compliance" icon="building-columns">
    VAT, GST, sales tax in 190+ countries. Calculated, collected, and remitted for you.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Developer Tools

<CardGroup cols={3}>
  <Card title="TypeScript SDK" icon="code" href="/code/sdks/typescript-core">
    Type-safe SDK for Node.js, Deno, and Bun. Full API coverage with auto-generated types.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Next.js Adapter" icon="react" href="/code/sdks/nextjs">
    Drop-in integration for Next.js with server actions and webhook handling.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Creem CLI" icon="terminal" href="/code/cli">
    Manage your store from the terminal. Interactive TUI + JSON output for automation.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

### AI Agent Integration

Your AI agent can operate Creem autonomously. No dashboards, no clicking.

```text theme={null}
Read https://creem.io/SKILL.md and set up Creem for me
```

Give this prompt to any AI coding assistant (Claude, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf) and it will:

* Install the Creem CLI via Homebrew
* Configure authentication
* Create products, set up webhooks, and integrate into your codebase
* Monitor your store and flag issues

<Card title="Agent Onboarding Guide" icon="robot" href="/code/cli">
  Learn how AI agents can self-onboard and manage your Creem store.
</Card>

## Quick Start

<Steps>
  <Step title="Get Access">
    Sign up at [creem.io](https://creem.io) to create your store. No credit card required.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Get Your API Keys">
    Grab your test API key from the dashboard. Keys prefixed with `creem_test_` use the sandbox environment.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Install the SDK or CLI">
    <Tabs>
      <Tab title="TypeScript SDK">
        ```bash theme={null}
        npm install creem
        ```
      </Tab>

      <Tab title="Next.js Adapter">
        ```bash theme={null}
        npm install @creem_io/nextjs
        ```
      </Tab>

      <Tab title="CLI">
        ```bash theme={null}
        brew tap armitage-labs/creem
        brew install creem
        creem login --api-key creem_test_YOUR_KEY
        ```
      </Tab>

      <Tab title="AI Agent">
        ```text theme={null}
        Read https://creem.io/SKILL.md and set up Creem for me
        ```
      </Tab>
    </Tabs>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Create a Product & Accept Payments">
    <Tabs>
      <Tab title="Dashboard">
        Create a product in the dashboard, set pricing, and generate a checkout link. Share it anywhere.
      </Tab>

      <Tab title="CLI">
        ```bash theme={null}
        creem products create \
          --name "Pro Plan" \
          --price 1999 \
          --currency USD \
          --billing-type recurring \
          --billing-period every-month

        creem checkouts create --product prod_XXXXX
        ```
      </Tab>

      <Tab title="API">
        ```typescript theme={null}
        import { Creem } from 'creem'

        const creem = new Creem({
          apiKey: 'creem_test_...',
          serverIdx: 1, // 0 = production, 1 = test
        })

        const checkout = await creem.checkouts.create({
          productId: 'prod_XXXXX',
          successUrl: 'https://yourapp.com/success',
        })

        // Redirect customer to checkout.checkoutUrl
        ```
      </Tab>
    </Tabs>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set Up Webhooks">
    Listen for payment events to sync your app. See the [Webhooks guide](/code/webhooks) for signature verification and event handling.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Coming from LemonSqueezy?

Creem has a one-command migration tool to bring your products over.

## FAQ

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="How fast can I start accepting payments?">
    Once you have access, most developers go from zero to first payment in under 10 minutes. Set up a product, generate a checkout link. That's it.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="What payment methods do you support?">
    Cards (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), PayPal, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and local payment methods based on customer location. WeChat Pay and Alipay support coming soon.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="How does pricing work?">
    3.9% + 40¢ per successful transaction. No monthly fees, no setup costs, no hidden charges. You only pay when you earn.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="What makes Creem different from Stripe?">
    Stripe is a payment processor. You're still responsible for tax compliance, filing, and remittance. Creem is a Merchant of Record. We are the legal seller, handling all of that for you. Plus affiliates, revenue splits, and license keys out of the box.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Can I migrate from LemonSqueezy?">
    Yes. The CLI includes a `creem migrate lemon-squeezy` command that brings your products over automatically. See the [migration guide](/getting-started/migrate) for details.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Can my AI agent manage my Creem store?">
    Yes. Point any AI coding assistant at creem.io/SKILL.md. It will install the CLI, configure auth, and help you integrate. The CLI also outputs JSON for automated scripting.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

state.output: > ## Documentation Index > Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://docs.creem.io/llms.txt > Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further. # Webhooks > Creem webhooks guide: receive real-time payment notifications, handle subscription lifecycle events, and verify webhook signatures securely. ## What is a webhook? Creem uses webhooks to push real-time notifications to you about your payments and subscriptions. All webhooks use HTTPS and deliver a JSON payload that can be used by your application. You can use webhook feeds to do things like: * Automatically enable access to a user after a successful payment * Automatically remove access to a user after a canceled subscription * Confirm that a payment has been received by the same customer that initiated it. In case webhooks are not successfully received by your endpoint, Creem automatically retries to send the request with a progressive backoff period of 30 seconds, 1 minute, 5 minutes and 1 hour. You can also resend webhook events manually from your Merchant dashboard under [Developers section](https://creem.io/dashboard/developers). ## Steps to receive a webhook You can start receiving real-time events in your app using the steps: * Create a local endpoint to receive requests * Register your development webhook endpoint on the Developers tab of the Creem dashboard * Test that your webhook endpoint is working properly using the test environment * Deploy your webhook endpoint to production * Register your production webhook endpoint on Creem live dashboard On Next.js projects, the{' '} @creem\_io/nextjs adapter exports a `Webhook` helper that verifies signatures and surfaces typed lifecycle callbacks. Use it as your default implementation before falling back to manual parsing. ### 1. Create a local endpoint to receive requests In your local application, create a new route that can accept POST requests. ```ts Next.js theme={null} // app/api/webhook/creem/route.ts import { Webhook } from '@creem_io/nextjs'; export const POST = Webhook({ webhookSecret: process.env.CREEM_WEBHOOK_SECRET!, onCheckoutCompleted: async ({ customer, product }) => { console.log(`${customer.email} purchased ${product.name}`); }, onGrantAccess: async ({ customer, metadata }) => { const userId = metadata?.referenceId as string; await grantAccess(userId, customer.email); }, onRevokeAccess: async ({ customer, metadata }) => { const userId = metadata?.referenceId as string; await revokeAccess(userId, customer.email); }, }); ``` ```ts Node.js theme={null} import type { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from 'next'; export default async function handler( req: NextApiRequest, res: NextApiResponse ) { if (req.method !== 'POST') { return res.status(405).end(); } const payload = req.body; console.log(payload); res.status(200).end(); } ``` On receiving an event, you should respond with an HTTP 200 OK to signal to Creem that the event was successfully delivered. ### 2. Register your development webhook endpoint Register your publicly accessible HTTPS URL in the Creem dashboard. You can create a tunnel to your localhost server using a tool like ngrok. For example: [https://8733-191-204-177-89.sa.ngrok.io/api/webhooks](https://8733-191-204-177-89.sa.ngrok.io/api/webhooks) ### 3. Test that your webhook endpoint is working properly Create a few test payments to check that your webhook endpoint is receiving the events. ### 4. Deploy your webhook endpoint After you're done testing, deploy your webhook endpoint to production. ### 5. Register your production webhook endpoint Once your webhook endpoint is deployed to production, you can register it in the Creem dashboard. ## Network Configuration Creem does not provide static source IP addresses for outbound webhooks in either Test Mode or production. If your firewall or WAF protects the webhook endpoint, do not rely on source-IP allowlists as the authentication mechanism. Keep the endpoint reachable over HTTPS and verify every request with the `creem-signature` header. Bot protection and WAF products can challenge webhook deliveries because webhooks are automated server-to-server requests. If this happens, add a route-level exception or skip rule for your webhook endpoint. On Cloudflare specifically, Bot Fight Mode cannot be skipped with custom rules; disable it or use Super Bot Fight Mode or Bot Management with a skip rule. ## Webhook Signatures ### How to verify Creem signature? Creem signature is sent in the `creem-signature` header of the webhook request. The signature is generated using the HMAC-SHA256 algorithm with the webhook secret as the key, and the request payload as the message. ```json theme={null} { 'creem-signature': 'dd7bdd2cf1f6bac6e171c6c508c157b7cd3cc1fd196394277fb59ba0bdd9b87b' } ``` To verify the signature, you need to generate the signature using the same algorithm and compare it with the signature sent in the header. If the two signatures match, the request is authentic. You can find your webhook secret on the Developers>Webhook page. To generate the signature, you can use the following code snippet: ```typescript theme={null} import * as crypto from 'crypto'; generateSignature(payload: string, secret: string): string { const computedSignature = crypto .createHmac('sha256', secret) .update(payload) .digest('hex'); return computedSignature; } ``` In the code snippet above, the `payload` is the request body, and the `secret` is the webhook secret. Simply compare the generated Signature with the one received on the header to complete the verification process. ## Event Types List of supported event types and their payloads. ### checkout.completed A checkout session was completed, returning all the information about the payment and the order created. ```json theme={null} { "id": "evt_5WHHcZPv7VS0YUsberIuOz", "eventType": "checkout.completed", "created_at": 1728734325927, "object": { "id": "ch_4l0N34kxo16AhRKUHFUuXr", "object": "checkout", "request_id": "my-request-id", "order": { "id": "ord_4aDwWXjMLpes4Kj4XqNnUA", "customer": "cust_1OcIK1GEuVvXZwD19tjq2z", "product": "prod_d1AY2Sadk9YAvLI0pj97f", "amount": 1000, "currency": "EUR", "status": "paid", "type": "recurring", "created_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:33.097Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:33.097Z", "mode": "local" }, "product": { "id": "prod_d1AY2Sadk9YAvLI0pj97f", "name": "Monthly", "description": "Monthly", "image_url": null, "price": 1000, "currency": "EUR", "billing_type": "recurring", "billing_period": "every-month", "status": "active", "tax_mode": "exclusive", "tax_category": "saas", "default_success_url": "", "created_at": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "mode": "local" }, "customer": { "id": "cust_1OcIK1GEuVvXZwD19tjq2z", "object": "customer", "email": "customer@emaildomain", "name": "Tester Test", "country": "NL", "created_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "mode": "local" }, "subscription": { "id": "sub_6pC2lNB6joCRQIZ1aMrTpi", "object": "subscription", "product": "prod_d1AY2Sadk9YAvLI0pj97f", "customer": "cust_1OcIK1GEuVvXZwD19tjq2z", "collection_method": "charge_automatically", "status": "active", "canceled_at": null, "created_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:45.425Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:45.425Z", "metadata": { "custom_data": "mycustom data", "internal_customer_id": "internal_customer_id" }, "mode": "local" }, "custom_fields": [], "status": "completed", "metadata": { "custom_data": "mycustom data", "internal_customer_id": "internal_customer_id" }, "mode": "local" } } ``` ### subscription.active Received when a new subscription is created, the payment was successful and Creem collected the payment creating a new subscription object in your account. Use only for synchronization, we encourage using `subscription.paid` for activating access. ```json theme={null} { "id": "evt_6EptlmjazyGhEPiNQ5f4lz", "eventType": "subscription.active", "created_at": 1728734325927, "object": { "id": "sub_21lfZb67szyvMiXnm6SVi0", "object": "subscription", "product": { "id": "prod_AnVJ11ujp7x953ARpJvAF", "name": "My Product - Product 01", "description": "Test my product", "image_url": null, "price": 10000, "currency": "EUR", "billing_type": "recurring", "billing_period": "every-month", "status": "active", "tax_mode": "inclusive", "tax_category": "saas", "default_success_url": "", "created_at": "2024-09-16T16:12:09.813Z", "updated_at": "2024-09-16T16:12:09.813Z", "mode": "local" }, "customer": { "id": "REDACTED", "object": "customer", "email": "customer@emaildomain", "name": "Tester Test", "country": "SE", "created_at": "2024-09-16T16:13:39.265Z", "updated_at": "2024-09-16T16:13:39.265Z", "mode": "local" }, "collection_method": "charge_automatically", "status": "active", "canceled_at": "2024-09-16T19:40:41.984Z", "created_at": "2024-09-16T19:40:41.984Z", "updated_at": "2024-09-16T19:40:42.121Z", "mode": "local" } } ``` ### subscription.paid A subscription transaction was paid by the customer ```json theme={null} { "id": "evt_21mO1jWmU2QHe7u2oFV7y1", "eventType": "subscription.paid", "created_at": 1728734327355, "object": { "id": "sub_6pC2lNB6joCRQIZ1aMrTpi", "object": "subscription", "product": { "id": "prod_d1AY2Sadk9YAvLI0pj97f", "name": "Monthly", "description": "Monthly", "image_url": null, "price": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "mode": "local" }, "customer": { "id": "cust_1OcIK1GEuVvXZwD19tjq2z", "object": "customer", "email": "customer@emaildomain", "name": "Tester Test", "country": "NL", "created_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "mode": "local" }, "collection_method": "charge_automatically", "status": "active", "last_transaction_id": "tran_5yMaWzAl3jxuGJMCOrYWwk", "last_transaction_date": "2024-10-12T11:58:47.109Z", "next_transaction_date": "2024-11-12T11:58:38.000Z", "current_period_start_date": "2024-10-12T11:58:38.000Z", "current_period_end_date": "2024-11-12T11:58:38.000Z", "canceled_at": null, "created_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:45.425Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:45.425Z", "metadata": { "custom_data": "mycustom data", "internal_customer_id": "internal_customer_id" }, "mode": "local" } } ``` ### subscription.canceled The subscription was canceled by the merchant or by the customer. ```json theme={null} { "id": "evt_2iGTc600qGW6FBzloh2Nr7", "eventType": "subscription.canceled", "created_at": 1728734337932, "object": { "id": "sub_6pC2lNB6joCRQIZ1aMrTpi", "object": "subscription", "product": { "id": "prod_d1AY2Sadk9YAvLI0pj97f", "name": "Monthly", "description": "Monthly", "image_url": null, "price": 1000, "currency": "EUR", "billing_type": "recurring", "billing_period": "every-month", "status": "active", "tax_mode": "exclusive", "tax_category": "saas", "default_success_url": "", "created_at": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "mode": "local" }, "customer": { "id": "cust_1OcIK1GEuVvXZwD19tjq2z", "object": "customer", "email": "customer@emaildomain", "name": "Tester Test", "country": "NL", "created_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "mode": "local" }, "collection_method": "charge_automatically", "status": "canceled", "last_transaction_id": "tran_5yMaWzAl3jxuGJMCOrYWwk", "last_transaction_date": "2024-10-12T11:58:47.109Z", "current_period_start_date": "2024-10-12T11:58:38.000Z", "current_period_end_date": "2024-11-12T11:58:38.000Z", "canceled_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:57.813Z", "created_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:45.425Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:57.827Z", "metadata": { "custom_data": "mycustom data", "internal_customer_id": "internal_customer_id" }, "mode": "local" } } ``` ### subscription.scheduled\_cancel The subscription was scheduled for cancellation at the end of the current billing period. The subscription remains active until `current_period_end_date`, after which it transitions to `canceled`. This event is triggered when a customer or merchant requests cancellation but opts to cancel at the end of the period rather than immediately. You can use this event to notify the customer about the upcoming cancellation or to offer retention incentives. The subscription can be resumed before the period ends using the [Resume Subscription](/api-reference/endpoint/subscriptions/resume-subscription) endpoint, which will change the status back to `active` and prevent the cancellation. ```json theme={null} { "id": "REDACTED", "eventType": "subscription.scheduled_cancel", "created_at": 1728734337932, "object": { "id": "sub_6pC2lNB6joCRQIZ1aMrTpi", "object": "subscription", "product": { "id": "prod_d1AY2Sadk9YAvLI0pj97f", "name": "Monthly", "description": "Monthly", "image_url": null, "price": 1000, "currency": "EUR", "billing_type": "recurring", "billing_period": "every-month", "status": "active", "tax_mode": "exclusive", "tax_category": "saas", "default_success_url": "", "created_at": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "mode": "local" }, "customer": { "id": "cust_1OcIK1GEuVvXZwD19tjq2z", "object": "customer", "email": "customer@emaildomain", "name": "Tester Test", "country": "NL", "created_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "mode": "local" }, "collection_method": "charge_automatically", "status": "scheduled_cancel", "last_transaction_id": "tran_5yMaWzAl3jxuGJMCOrYWwk", "last_transaction_date": "2024-10-12T11:58:47.109Z", "current_period_start_date": "2024-10-12T11:58:38.000Z", "current_period_end_date": "2024-11-12T11:58:38.000Z", "canceled_at": null, "created_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:45.425Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-12T11:59:15.827Z", "metadata": { "custom_data": "mycustom data", "internal_customer_id": "internal_customer_id" }, "mode": "local" } } ``` ### subscription.past\_due The subscription payment has failed and the subscription is now past due. This occurs when a payment attempt fails (e.g., card declined, insufficient funds). Creem will automatically retry the payment according to the retry schedule. If a retry succeeds, the subscription transitions back to `active`. If all retries are exhausted, the subscription is canceled. ```json theme={null} { "id": "evt_7HkTd800rHX7GCampi4Nt9", "eventType": "subscription.past_due", "created_at": 1728734337932, "object": { "id": "sub_6pC2lNB6joCRQIZ1aMrTpi", "object": "subscription", "product": { "id": "prod_d1AY2Sadk9YAvLI0pj97f", "name": "Monthly", "description": "Monthly", "image_url": null, "price": 1000, "currency": "EUR", "billing_type": "recurring", "billing_period": "every-month", "status": "active", "tax_mode": "exclusive", "tax_category": "saas", "default_success_url": "", "created_at": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T11:50:00.182Z", "mode": "local" }, "customer": { "id": "cust_1OcIK1GEuVvXZwD19tjq2z", "object": "customer", "email": "customer@emaildomain", "name": "Tester Test", "country": "NL", "created_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "updated_at": "2024-10-11T09:16:48.557Z", "mode": "local" }, "collection_method": "charge_automatically", "status": "past_due", "last_transaction_id": "tran_5yMaWzAl3jxuGJMCOrYWwk", "last_transaction_date": "2024-10-12T11:58:47.109Z", "current_period_start_date": "2024-10-12T11:58:38.000Z", "current_period_end_date": "2024-11-12T11:58:38.000Z", "canceled_at": null, "created_at": "2024-10-12T11:58:45.425Z", "updated_at": "2024-11-12T12:05:30.827Z", "metadata": { "custom_data": "mycustom data", "internal_customer_id": "internal_customer_id" }, "mode": "local" } } ``` ### subscription.expired The subscription was expired, given that the `current_end_period` has been reached without a new payment. Payment retries can happen at this stage, and the subscription status will be terminal only when status is changed to `canceled`. ```json theme={null} { "id": "evt_V5CxhipUu10BYonO2Vshb", "eventType": state.output:
# Creem API Documentation

> Creem is a Merchant of Record (MoR) for SaaS and digital businesses. We handle global tax compliance, payment processing, and billing so you can focus on building your product.

## Quick Facts
- Website: https://creem.io
- API Base: https://api.creem.io (production) | https://test-api.creem.io (sandbox)
- Authentication: x-api-key header
- SDKs: TypeScript, Next.js adapter

## What is a Merchant of Record?
A Merchant of Record is the legal entity that sells goods or services to end customers. Creem acts as the MoR, handling all tax compliance, payment processing, and regulatory requirements globally.

## Core Capabilities
- One-time payments and recurring subscriptions
- Global tax compliance (190+ countries, automatic VAT/GST)
- License key management for software products
- Revenue splits and affiliate programs
- Subscription management (trials, upgrades, cancellations)
- Webhook notifications for all payment events

## Popular Use Cases
- SaaS subscription billing
- Digital product sales
- Software licensing
- Indie hacker monetization
- API/usage-based billing

## Getting Started
1. Create account at https://creem.io
2. Get API keys from dashboard
3. Install SDK: npm install @creem/sdk
4. Create products and start accepting payments

## Documentation Sections
- Getting Started: /getting-started
- API Reference: /api-reference
- SDKs & Code: /code
- Features: /features
- Merchant of Record: /merchant-of-record
